title: Toolchain Selection, Compatibility, and Reproducible Setup · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Select a minimal compatible toolchain and prove clean-machine setup. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a pinned bootstrap and compatibility matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pin supported runtime and package-manager versions with one authoritative source.
Commit appropriate lockfiles and verify deterministic clean installs.
Choose tools with clear ownership and minimal duplicate diagnostics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Runtime Version evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Runtime Version assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Pin supported runtime and package-manager versions with one authoritative source. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: gates toolchain. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Runtime Version, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a pinned bootstrap and compatibility matrix.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Latest floating versions produce reproducible builds. This fails the Runtime Version contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Clang AddressSanitizer and UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer (Clang 23 development documentation) — Controls native-code runtime sanitizer purpose, build profiles, output, limitations, and security cautions.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pin supported runtime and package-manager versions with one authoritative source.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

options.text: Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Bootstrap Smoke applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test setup, build, and checks from a clean environment.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
